{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 3,
    "R1.2": 3,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Identifies one conceptual issue (Lipschitz contradiction) and proposes a tighter framing, partially supported.",
    "R1.2": "Engages the argument chain but does not walk end-to-end; flags some consistency issues with localized fixes.",
    "R1.4": "Names one prior work (Locatello et al.) and articulates the delta, but could map against more works for a clearer picture.",
    "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (identifiability).",
    "RX.3": "Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (missing citations).",
    "RX.5": "Output blends sources without traceability."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.8,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.6,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 0.5
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: Identifies one conceptual issue (Lipschitz contradiction) and proposes a tighter framing, partially supported.
rationale.R1.2: Engages the argument chain but does not walk end-to-end; flags some consistency issues with localized fixes.
rationale.R1.4: Names one prior work (Locatello et al.) and articulates the delta, but could map against more works for a clearer picture.
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (identifiability).
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (missing citations).
